ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for bacon and mushroom Alfredo pasta; FOODON_00002473 (pasta dish) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Pasta dishes — composite cooked pasta with sauce
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers composite pasta dishes with sauce; no specific Alfredo with bacon and mushroom code exists. Closest parent category applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific composite pasta dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant plate of Bacon and Mushroom Alfredo Pasta weighs approximately 400 g, providing around 950 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pasta with cream sauce, meat, and vegetables (composite estimate); supplemented by USDA SR Legacy entries for cooked pasta, bacon, mushrooms, heavy cream, butter, and Parmesan cheese
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Bacon and Mushroom Alfredo Pasta as a composite dish. Values are estimated from a weighted blend of USDA SR Legacy entries: cooked enriched pasta (~40% by weight), smoked bacon (~15%), mixed mushrooms (~15%), heavy whipping cream (~15%), butter (~5%), and Parmesan cheese (~10%). The input record's per_100g values (energy 237.5 kcal, protein 8.75 g, fat 16.25 g, carbohydrate 13.75 g) are consistent with this USDA-derived composite estimate and have been retained with cross-validation. Sodium 300 mg/100g is plausible given bacon and cheese contributions. All values are classified as estimated.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entry for pasta with cream sauce
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bacon and Mushroom Alfredo Pasta. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for cooked pasta with cream-based sauce (GI range 40–50 for al dente pasta; fat and protein from cream, bacon, and cheese further attenuate glycaemic response). Glycemic load per 400 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (45 × 55 g) / 100 = 24.75, rounded to 24.6. This is classified as medium GL per serving. Analogous estimate basis documented per Veritas policy.

hypertension.key_factor: High saturated fat (8.75 g/100g) and moderate sodium (300 mg/100g) from smoked bacon, Parmigiano Reggiano, and butter
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to saturated fat exceeding 5 g/100g threshold and borderline sodium; if consumed, reduce bacon and cheese quantities and substitute cream with low-fat milk to lower both saturated fat and sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined pasta carbohydrate (13.75 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.0 g/100g) and high saturated fat worsening insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit this dish; substituting refined pasta with wholemeal or legume-based pasta increases fibre and reduces glycaemic impact, while reducing cream and butter lowers saturated fat that compounds insulin resistance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 8.75 g/100g, trans fat at 0.38 g/100g, and cholesterol at 45 mg/100g from cream, butter, bacon, and Parmesan cheese
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid or strictly limit this dish; the combination of high saturated fat, trans fat, and dietary cholesterol from cream, butter, smoked bacon, and Parmigiano Reggiano significantly elevates LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat at 8.75 g/100g from butter and cream triggers pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6); smoked bacon is a processed meat with additional pro-inflammatory properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish; substituting butter and cream with olive oil and low-fat milk, and replacing smoked bacon with grilled chicken or additional mushrooms, would substantially reduce the pro-inflammatory saturated fat and arachidonic acid load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bacon and Mushroom Alfredo Pasta is a rich, indulgent Italian-style pasta dish popular in Singapore's casual dining and food delivery market. It consists of cooked pasta — typically fettuccine or spaghetti — tossed in a creamy Alfredo sauce made from heavy cream, butter, and Parmigiano Reggiano cheese, combined with pan-fried smoked bacon and a medley of sautéed mushrooms. The dish has a velvety, umami-rich flavour profile with smoky notes from the bacon and earthy depth from the mushrooms. The texture is creamy and coating, with tender pasta strands and slightly chewy bacon pieces. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 237.5 kcal, 8.75 g protein, 16.25 g fat (of which 8.75 g is saturated), and 13.75 g carbohydrate with only 1.0 g dietary fibre. The high saturated fat content from cream, butter, and cheese makes this dish a high-concern food for individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. Sodium at 300 mg/100g is borderline elevated due to bacon and aged cheese. The glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low) due to fat and protein attenuating the glycaemic response of the pasta, though the glycemic load per standard 400 g plate is approximately 24.6 (medium). This dish is not suitable for vegetarian, vegan, halal, or gluten-free diets. It contains gluten (pasta), dairy (cream, butter, Parmesan), and sulphites (smoked bacon). It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary staple.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Bacon and Mushroom Alfredo Pasta as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted USDA FoodData Central component blend (cooked pasta, smoked bacon, mushrooms, heavy cream, butter, Parmesan cheese). The input record values are consistent with this estimate and have been retained. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database pasta-with-cream-sauce entries. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite pasta dish is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026-04-16; component-level fatty acid data would require a full recipe-level calculation beyond the scope of this record. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific composite dish are not reported as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026-04-16. trans_fat_g value of 0.38 g/100g is retained from input; this is plausible given butter and cream contributions (naturally occurring ruminant trans fats). The dish is not halal due to pork bacon. The ethnic_tags entry 'Pizza' in the input has been removed as it is not applicable to this pasta dish. country_of_origin in the input was listed as Singapore, reflecting the GrabFood delivery context; region_of_origin is documented as Italy (served in Singapore) to reflect culinary origin accurately. GI glycemic load per serving calculated as (45 × 55 g carbohydrate per 400 g serving) / 100 = 24.75, rounded to 24.6. Iteration 3 fix: ontology foodon_match_type and foodex2_match_type corrected from 'broader' to 'closest_parent' to conform to the allowed enumeration values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] as required by g5_ontology_honesty gate.
